articles.title: Adani Defence breaks ground on ₹2,500-crore missile ecosystem in Madhya Pradesh
articles.summary: The project is expected to create 5,000 direct and indirect skilled jobs, opening new avenues for micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) while cementing Madhya Pradesh's position as a strategic defense manufacturing hub.

articles.title: 15 India-bound ships carrying urea, DAP and sulphur cross Hormuz: Govt
articles.summary: Despite Middle East conflict disruptions, India's fertilizer supply remains robust. Most shipments successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz, while domestic production surpassed targets.

articles.title: OPEC+ agrees fresh August oil output hike as Strait of Hormuz reopens
articles.summary: OPEC+ is boosting oil output by another 188,000 barrels daily from August, continuing to reverse earlier cuts. Despite this, actual supply remains below pre-conflict levels due to disruptions impacting key producers.

articles.title: From 'monstrously stupid' to mainstream fuel: Has ethanol proved legendary Charlie Munger wrong?
articles.summary: Nearly two decades later, his criticism continues to fuel debate — not just over the science of ethanol's energy balance, but also over its economic viability, food security implications and role in the transition to cleaner energy.

articles.title: Billionaire's reading list: Zerodha's Nikhil Kamath rates 5 books guiding his life & strategy
articles.summary: For a billionaire deeply rooted in the numbers-driven world of stockbroking, the revelation that human ambition is largely an unconscious attempt to escape mortality shifted his entire perspective.

articles.title: 'No such proposal for exporting E20 petrol': Centre dismisses Bhutan E20 rejection claims
articles.summary: The Petroleum Ministry has debunked claims of Bhutan refusing India's E20 petrol, stating no such offer was ever made. This clarification follows opposition allegations that several neighboring countries rejected the fuel.

articles.title: US tops the global economic chart—but for how long? Deutsche Bank flags biggest risk
articles.summary: America's mounting debt is now the primary threat to its global economic dominance, surpassing external competition. Deutsche Bank warns that persistent deficits and soaring interest payments are eroding a key advantage.

articles.title: Dalal Street outlook: TCS, crude oil and more, what's driving stock market this week?
articles.summary: Indian markets brace for a pivotal week as the June-quarter earnings season kicks off with TCS on July 9. Investors will scrutinize results and management commentary on demand and AI.

articles.title: Political stability beats high returns: The world's safest countries for investors in 2026
articles.summary: Rather than chasing high-growth opportunities, these economies offer investors greater confidence that their capital will remain protected during periods of global uncertainty.

articles.title: India's top IT firms set for muted Q1 as AI spending fails to lift growth: Report
articles.summary: India's top IT firms anticipate a slow start to FY27, with growth likely to remain subdued as clients focus on cost savings over major tech investments. While AI adoption is rising, it's funded by efficiency gains, not budget increases.

articles.title: Pakistan's public money a privilege? World Bank report reveals stark fiscal faultlines
articles.summary: Pakistan's fiscal decentralisation efforts have failed to bridge regional spending gaps, with provincial capitals like Quetta and Lahore receiving significantly more per person than their own provinces. Despite budget surpluses in some areas, poorer regions face underinvestment.

articles.title: Market recap: Top-10 firms add Rs 1 lakh crore in market capitalisation; Airtel, Bajaj Finance lead gains
articles.summary: Dalal Street concluded the past week on a high note, with top companies collectively adding over Rs 1 lakh crore to their market cap. Bharti Airtel and Bajaj Finance led the gains, buoyed by strong domestic economic data and positive global sentiment.

articles.title: 'Adani case should never have been brought': US Justice Department urges judge to permanently drop charges
articles.summary: In a 10-page filing, the department said the indictment "should have been dropped a year ago -- or never brought in the first place," urging US District Judge Nicholas Garaufis to dismiss the case with prejudice

articles.title: India's passport ranking slips, remains outside top 100: Which nations are in top 10?
Quote of the Day by JRD Tata: ‘Most of our troubles are due to...’
articles.summary: JRD Tata was a legendary stickler for perfection. He believed that building a world-class enterprise could not be accomplished overnight or through superficial methods.

articles.title: India misses top 50 as Switzerland ranks world's safest country for investors in 2026
articles.summary: India has not made it to the list of the world's 50 safest countries for investors in 2026, according to the Global Investment Risk and Resilience Score 2026 by Henley & Partners.

articles.title: Ex-CFO of RCap, Amit Bapna, held in connection with ADAG probe
articles.summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has apprehended Amit Bapna, former CFO of Reliance Capital Ltd, as part of its probe into Reliance ADA Group matters.

articles.title: India-UK trade pact: Centre notifies rules for determination of origin of goods
articles.summary: India's Finance Ministry has finalized rules for goods eligible for benefits under the India-UK trade pact, set to begin July 15.

articles.title: 'Criticism is welcome, rumours are not': Puri on E20 fuel buzz, engine damage claims
articles.summary: Union Minister Hardeep Singh Puri has addressed social media concerns regarding ethanol-blended fuel, dismissing claims of pest attraction and engine damage. He emphasized that ethanol usage is a century-old concept and highlighted past government efforts.

articles.title: Chip by chip: India now world's second largest phone maker; PM Modi lays out 'Make in India' electronics roadmap
articles.summary: India has ascended to become the world's second-largest mobile phone manufacturer and exporter, Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced, underscoring the nation's commitment to building a comprehensive electronics and semiconductor ecosystem.

articles.title: Is E20 fuel safe for your vehicle? Here's what experts say
articles.summary: Concerns surrounding E20 ethanol-blended petrol are being addressed by industry experts, who assure its safety for all vehicles, including older models. Years of rigorous scientific testing and consultations with automakers have confirmed its efficacy.

articles.title: Adani Group’s aluminium play gives it a strategic advantage in power businesses, say analysts
articles.summary: The mega $11.5 billion project is an opportunity to bolster its presence in commodities.

articles.title: Centre raises onion procurement price by 13% — here's why
articles.summary: The government has increased its onion procurement price to Rs 2,125 per quintal, a 13% jump, to boost farmer earnings and accelerate buffer stock building. This marks the fifth revision this season amidst sluggish procurement.

articles.title: Powered by sweat: Why Japan's latest innovation could reshape wearable tech
articles.summary: Unlike conventional batteries, the device generates energy only when sweat is available, making it particularly suited for wearable sensors used during exercise or continuous health monitoring.

articles.title: America spends over $1 billion on July 4 fireworks: The story behind the dazzling spectacle
articles.summary: The first official Independence Day fireworks display was held in Philadelphia, where residents celebrated the nation's first anniversary with fireworks, bonfires, music and public festivities.

articles.title: Beyond Hormuz: What HPCL Rajasthan Refinery Limited means for India's energy future
articles.summary: India is bolstering its energy security following the Strait of Hormuz disruption. Prime Minister Modi inaugurated the HPCL Rajasthan Refinery, a crucial step towards self-reliance for a nation heavily reliant on oil imports.

articles.title: July 4 coincidence: 3 US Presidents died on this day, including founding fathers John Adams & Thomas Jefferson
articles.summary: Adams and Jefferson worked closely during the Revolutionary War and both served on the committee that drafted the Declaration of Independence, with Jefferson writing the first draft and Adams helping steer it through debate.

articles.title: India-Israel BIA decoded: What's in it for businesses and investors?
articles.summary: The India-Israel Bilateral Investment Agreement (BIA), signed in September 2025, has come into force from July 4, 2026, replacing the nearly three-decade-old investment treaty between the two countries.

articles.title: Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train: India to get its 1st high-speed rail service by 2027; check key features, travel time & more
articles.summary: India's first bullet train is set to begin its journey by 2027, with the Surat to Bilimora section slated for inauguration. Union Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw announced that construction on the Mumbai-Ahmedabad corridor has surpassed 80% completion.

articles.title: Year after calling India 'dead economy', Trump says country 'doing very well at 7-8%'
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has lauded India's robust economic growth, a significant departure from his past criticisms. He cited India's 7-8% growth as an example of strong performance that shouldn't be stifled by high interest rates.

articles.title: India eyes $1 trillion exports milestone: Amid global turmoil, which sectors will drive growth? Decoded
articles.summary: During the April-June quarter, exports saw a 15% growth in merchandise shipments and around 11% in services exports. Which sectors have contributed to exports growth? What role has the PLI scheme played and will trade deals help expedite growth? We decode:

articles.title: India allows four Chinese firms into critical power tenders; Congress attacks move
articles.summary: India has permitted four Chinese power equipment manufacturers with local production to bid on critical government power projects for two years. This relaxation, aimed at accelerating power network expansion and renewable energy integration, follows a power ministry request.

articles.title: Fuel prices explained: Why oil companies are still losing ₹19 on diesel and ₹6 on petrol
articles.summary: State-run oil marketing companies (OMCs) lost an estimated ₹18.9 on every litre of diesel and ₹6 on every litre of petrol during the April-June quarter, even as pump prices remained largely unchanged.

articles.title: ‘It’s an epidemic now!’: Tesla batteries keep getting stolen; here’s what’s happening
articles.summary: Law enforcement officials are particularly concerned by the rise of so-called strategic thefts, in which organised groups exploit weaknesses in supply chain security rather than stealing unattended trailers.

articles.title: Karnataka HC directs Swiggy, Zomato, Zepto and others to deposit gig workers' welfare fee within 3 weeks
articles.summary: Refusing to grant an interim stay on the Karnataka Gig Workers Welfare Act, the Karnataka High Court has directed Swiggy, Zomato, Zepto and other app-based aggregators to deposit the disputed welfare fee with the court within three weeks.

articles.title: Online food delivery isn't slowing! Why Indians are ordering more than ever
articles.summary: Online food delivery is set to become one of the biggest growth engines of India's food services industry, with consumers ordering more frequently than ever before.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by ace investor Rakesh Jhunjhunwala: 'I'd be lying if I...'
articles.summary: Rakesh Jhunjhunwala (1960-2022) was a legendary Indian billionaire investor and stock trader often referred to as the “Big Bull” of India or the “Indian Warren Buffett”.
